title: Educational Assessment Tools for IRT Modeling using Conquest or TAM
text: eatModel (Educational Assessment Tools IRT Modeling) provides functions compute IRT models (Rasch, 2PL, partial credit, generalized partial credit) using software Conquest R packages TAM mirt.

title: Educational Assessment Tools for IRT Modeling using Conquest or TAM
code: # Install eatRep from GitHub via remotes::install_github("weirichs/eatModel", upgrade="never")

title: Educational Assessment Tools for IRT Modeling using Conquest or TAM
code: library(eatModel) ### View package documentation package?eatModel

title: Educational Assessment Tools for IRT Modeling using Conquest or TAM
text: package comes two examplary data sets, one dichotomous items, one also polytomous items. First data set (trends) contains fictional achievement scores 13524 students two domains (reading, listening) three measurement occasions (2010, 2015, 2020) long format. data longitudinal individuals stem different cohorts overlap across measurement occasions. name data set trends. can find detailed exemplary analyses help pages defineModel(). following example estimates -item two-dimensional 2pl model conditioning (background) model student cohort measured 2020. splitModels() function can used previously corresponding model estimated 2010 2015 likewise.
code: library(eatModel) ### load exemplary data  data(trends)  ### choose data for 2020 and prepare data in the wide format dat2020<- reshape2::dcast(subset ( trends, year == 2020),           idstud+country+sex+ses+language~item, value.var="value")  ### create Q matrix indicating which item belongs to which latent dimension.  qMat   <- unique(trends[ ,c("item","domain")]) qMat   <- data.frame ( qMat[,"item", drop=FALSE], model.matrix(~domain-1, data = qMat))  ### specify the model def    <- defineModel(dat = dat2020, id = "idstud", items = colnames(dat2020)[-c(1:5)],           qMatrix = qMat, HG.var = c("sex", "ses", "language"), irtmodel = "2PL", software = "tam")  ### run the model run    <- runModel(def)  ### collect the results results<- getResults(run)  ### extract item parameters from results items  <- itemFromRes(results)  ### extract WLEs from results wles   <- wleFromREs(results)

title: Educational Assessment Tools for IRT Modeling using Conquest or TAM
text: second exemplary data set named reading contains dichotomous well polytomous items large-scale reading competence assessment. following demonstate restricted generalized partial credit model (GPCM) using TAM. define arbitrarily items dedicated students special educational needs (SEN) different slope parameter assumed.
code: # load partial credit long format data data(reading)  # transform into wide format datW <- reshape2::dcast(reading[which(reading[,"type"] != "iglu"),],         uniqueID+sex+language+country~item, value.var = "valueSum")  # combine the two lowest categories for item D205143 to avoid categories with too few observations datW[,"D205143"] <- car::recode(datW[,"D205143"], "1=0; 2=1; 3=2; 4=3; 5=4")  # restricted generalized partial credit model ... we consider the female subgroup to be the reference population # we assume that task D223 and D224 are created for students with special educational need and therefore may have # different slope. Non-SPF items (dichotomous as well as partial credit) should have slope = 1 # data for females dFema<- datW[which(datW[,"sex"] == "female"),]                               ### reference population mean and SD    items<- colnames(dFema)[-c(1:4)]  # create an item information data.frame which contain the task an item belongs to, the common slope group, # and the fixed slope parameter for 1pl items  items<- data.frame(item = items, task = substr(items,1,4),                     slopeGrp = as.numeric(substr(items,1,4) \%in\% c("D223", "D224")), stringsAsFactors = FALSE) |>          dplyr::mutate(slope = car::recode(slopeGrp, "0=1; else=NA")) items[,"dim"] <- car::recode(substr(items[,"item"], 1,2),"'D0'='norm'; 'D2'='pilot'")  # add the group (pilot, norm) each item belongs to  items<- data.frame ( items, model.matrix(~dim-1, data = items), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  # define a retsricted generalized partial credit model. Dichotomous items which  # are constrained to have a slope of 1 are modeled as Rasch items  def1T<- defineModel(dat=dFema, items = items[,"item"], id=1, irtmodel = "GPCM.groups",          software="tam", fixSlopeMat = na.omit(items[,c("item", "slope")]),          est.slopegroups = items[,c("item", "slopeGrp")], nodes = 21,          fac.oldxsi =0.6, increment.factor=1.05) run1T<- runModel(def1T) res1T<- getResults(run1T) it1T <- itemFromRes(res1T)  # males are focus group: initial free estimation of item parameters for males  def2T<- defineModel(dat=datW[which(datW[,"sex"] == "male"),], items = -c(1:4), id=1,          irtmodel = "GPCM.groups",software="tam", fixSlopeMat = na.omit(items[,c("item", "slope")]),          est.slopegroups = items[,c("item", "slopeGrp")], nodes = 21) run2T<- runModel(def2T) res2T<- getResults(run2T) it2T <- itemFromRes(res2T)  # average discrimination (reg vs. spf) differs for males, but not for females unique(it1T[,"estSlope"]); unique(it2T[,"estSlope"])                              # use only slope=1 items for 1pl linking eq   <- equat1pl(results = res2T, prmNorm = it1T[which(it1T[,"estSlope"] ==1),],          item = "item", cat="category", value = "est", difBound=.64, iterativ = TRUE)  # variant 1 # use the DIF-cleaned set of original (female) anchor parameters for calibrat### reference population mean and SDing males on the reference scale weg  <- eq[["items"]][["not_specified"]][["Dim1"]][["info"]][-1,"itemExcluded"] weg  <- eatTools::whereAre(weg, paste(it1T[,"item"], it1T[,"category"], sep="_")) it1C <- subset(it1T[-weg,], estSlope == 1) def3T<- defineModel(eatTools::na_omit_selection(dat=datW[which(datW[,"sex"] == "male"),],"language"),          items = -c(1:4), id=1, irtmodel = "GPCM.groups", anchor = it1C, HG.var = c("language", "country"),          itemCol = "item", valueCol = "est", catCol = "category", fixSlopeMat = na.omit(items[,c("item", "slope")]),         qMatrix = items[,c("item", "dimnorm", "dimpilot")], est.slopegroups = items[,c("item", "slopeGrp")],          nodes = 21, software="tam") run3T<- runModel(def3T) res3T<- getResults(run3T) it3T <- itemFromRes(res3T)                                                        # all items except the ones with linking dif with equal item parameters? check  comp <- merge(subset(it1T[,c("item", "category", "est", "estSlope")],estSlope == 1),                it3T[,c("item", "category", "est", "offset")], by=c("item", "category"),                all=TRUE, suffixes = c("_ref", "_foc")) equal<- na.omit(comp[,c("est_ref", "offset")]) ### reference population mean and SD # all item parameters without linking dif should be equal stopifnot(all(equal[,1] == equal[,2]))                                           lDif <- subset(comp, !is.na(est_foc)) stopifnot(all(paste(subset(lDif, !is.na(est_ref))[,"item"],            subset(lDif, !is.na(est_ref))[,"category"], sep="_") \%in\% eq$items[["not_specified"]][["Dim1"]][["info"]][,"itemExcluded"]))  # variant 2: use the 1pl item parameters for males (linking dif items excluded), transformed to the metric of females def4T<- defineModel(eatTools::na_omit_selection(dat=datW[which(datW[,"sex"] == "male"),],"language"),          items = -c(1:4), id=1, irtmodel = "GPCM.groups",           anchor = eq[["items"]][["not_specified"]][["Dim1"]][["cleanedLinkItemPars"]],         HG.var = c("language", "country"), itemCol = "item", valueCol = "est",          catCol = "category", fixSlopeMat = na.omit(items[,c("item", "slope")]),         qMatrix = items[,c("item", "dimnorm", "dimpilot")],          est.slopegroups = items[,c("item", "slopeGrp")], nodes = 21, software="tam") run4T<- runModel(def4T) res4T<- getResults(run4T) it4T <- itemFromRes(res4T)                                                        ### all items except the ones with linking dif with equal item parameters? check  link <- eq[["items"]][["not_specified"]][["Dim1"]][["cleanedLinkItemPars"]][,c("item", "category", "est")] comp <- merge(link, it4T[,c("item", "category", "est", "offset")], by=c("item", "category"), suffixes = c("_ref", "_foc"), all=TRUE)  # all item parameters without linking dif should be equal equal<- na.omit(comp[,c("est_ref", "offset")]) stopifnot(all(equal[,1] == equal[,2]))                                            # all items with specific focus paraeter must be included in linking DIF exclusion list  lDif <- subset(comp, !is.na(est_foc))                                            stopifnot(all(paste(subset(lDif, !is.na(est_ref))[,"item"],            subset(lDif, !is.na(est_ref))[,"category"], sep="_") \%in\% eq$items[["not_specified"]][["Dim1"]][["info"]][,"itemExcluded"]))  # transform to Bista metric eq4  <- equat1pl(results = res4T)                                                 # reference population mean and SD refP <- data.frame(domain = c("dimnorm", "dimpilot"), m = 0.0389, sd = 1.07108, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) cuts <- list ( dimnorm = list(values = 390+0:3*75), dimpilot = list(values = 390+0:3*75)) tf4  <- transformToBista(equatingList=eq4, refPop=refP, cuts=cuts, vera = FALSE)

what: check for consistency of context variables and items — checkContextVars
title: check for consistency of context variables and items — checkContextVars
text: Function checks whether types context vars, .e. group, DIF weighting variables, consistent item variables. function mainly used internal consistency checks.

title: check for consistency of context variables and items — checkContextVars
code: checkContextVars (x, varname, type = c("weight", "DIF", "group", "HG"), itemdata,                   suppressAbort = FALSE, internal = FALSE)

title: check for consistency of context variables and items — checkContextVars
text: x vector values context variable (e.g., DIF variable) varname Optional: name context variable type Type context variable following entries allowed: DIF, group, HG, weight. itemdata data.frame item responses suppressAbort Logical: function suppress abort inconsistencies occur? internal Logical: used internal use. Recommend set FALSE.

title: check for consistency of context variables and items — checkContextVars
text: list

title: check for consistency of context variables and items — checkContextVars
code: data(trends) # first reshape the data for the first time of measurement set into wide format datW <- reshape2::dcast(trends[which(trends[,"year"] == 2010),],                         idstud+sex+ses+language~item, value.var="value") chk1 <- checkContextVars(datW[,"language"], "language", type="DIF",                          itemdata = datW[,-c(1:4)]) #> Warning: Following 14 items are constants in DIF variable 'language', group other: #>    T01_01, T05_04, T07_04, T07_07, T07_08, T07_10, T09_04, T09_05, T09_06, T10_08, T12_05, T13_06, T15_10, T16_04 #> Warning: row names were found from a short variable and have been discarded chk1$info #>     varname varlevel nCases     type   vars value nValue #> 1  language    other     41 constant T01_01     1      9 #> 2  language    other     41 constant T05_04     1      8 #> 3  language    other     41 constant T07_04     0      5 #> 4  language    other     41 constant T07_07     0      5 #> 5  language    other     41 constant T07_08     1      5 #> 6  language    other     41 constant T07_10     0      5 #> 7  language    other     41 constant T09_04     0      5 #> 8  language    other     41 constant T09_05     1      5 #> 9  language    other     41 constant T09_06     1      5 #> 10 language    other     41 constant T10_08     0      9 #> 11 language    other     41 constant T12_05     1     14 #> 12 language    other     41 constant T13_06     0     13 #> 13 language    other     41 constant T15_10     0     17 #> 14 language    other     41 constant T16_04     1      9

title: check for linked design — checkLinking
text: Function checks whether blocks complete incomplete block design linked .

title: check for linked design — checkLinking
code: checkLinking(design, blocks=NULL, bookletColumn=NULL, verbose=FALSE)

title: check for linked design — checkLinking
text: design data frame test design. columns (except booklet identifier column) expected contain blocks. blocks Optional. check whether subdomain completely linked add blocks belong subdomain character vector. bookletColumn Optional. Number name booklet identifier column design data frame. verbose Optional. TRUE function gives messages.

title: check for linked design — checkLinking
text: list. Containing information linking diagnostics: completelyLinked logical value (TRUE/FALSE). Whether design completely linked. occuringBlockCombinations data.frame containing occureng frequencies block pairs. blockPositions data.frame containing information often block occurs position.

title: check for linked design — checkLinking
text: Sebastian Weirich Karoline Sachse

title: check for linked design — checkLinking
code: # 1. first examples # a) design linked des1a <- data.frame(booklet = paste0("B", 1:4),        Pos1 = c("blockA", "blockB", "blockC", "blockD"),        Pos2 = c("blockB", "blockC", "blockD", "blockE"),        Pos3 = c("blockC", "blockD", "blockE", "blockF")) test1 <- checkLinking(design = des1a, bookletColumn = "booklet", verbose=TRUE) #> Dataset is completely linked.  # b) design not linked: des1b <- data.frame(Pos1 = c("blockA", "blockH", "blockC", "blockF"),        Pos2 = c("blockB", "blockC", "blockD", "blockA"),        Pos3 = c("blockF", "blockG", "blockH", "blockB")) test2 <- checkLinking(design = des1b, verbose=TRUE) #> WARNING! Dataset is not completely linked. #> W A R N I N G !   Dataset is NOT completely linked (even if cases with missings on all items are removed). #>                   24 cases unconnected. Following items are unconnected:  #>                   blockH_1, blockH_2, blockH_3, blockC_1, blockC_2, blockC_3, blockG_1, blockG_2, blockG_3, blockD_1, blockD_2, blockD_3  # 2. second examples: use a 'IQB Bildungstrend 2016'-like design data(des2)  # design contains three dimensions -- reading, listening and orthography # linking check must be conducted for each dimension separately.  # a) domain reading (blocks contain "-L") readblocks <- grep("-L", unique(unlist(des2[,-1])), value=TRUE) test3 <- checkLinking(design = des2, blocks =readblocks, bookletColumn = "TH", verbose=TRUE) #> Special characters and spaces will be removed from names in 'blocks' #> WARNING! Dataset is not completely linked. #> W A R N I N G !   Dataset is NOT completely linked (even if cases with missings on all items are removed). #>                   48 cases unconnected. Following items are unconnected:  #>                   DL32_1, DL32_2, DL32_3  # b) domain listening (blocks contain "-H") listenblocks <- grep("-H", unique(unlist(des2[,-1])), value=TRUE) test4 <- checkLinking(design = des2[,-1], blocks =listenblocks, verbose=TRUE) #> Special characters and spaces will be removed from names in 'blocks' #> Dataset is completely linked.  # c) domain orthography (blocks contain "-R") orthoblocks <- grep("-R", unique(unlist(des2[,-1])), value=TRUE) test5 <- checkLinking(design = des2[,-1], blocks =orthoblocks, verbose=TRUE) #> Special characters and spaces will be removed from names in 'blocks' #> WARNING! Dataset is not completely linked. #> W A R N I N G !   Dataset is NOT completely linked (even if cases with missings on all items are removed). #>                   156 cases unconnected. Following items are unconnected:  #>                   DR13_1, DR13_2, DR13_3, DR01_1, DR01_2, DR01_3, DR23_1, DR23_2, DR23_3, DR0516S_1, DR0516S_2, DR0516S_3, DR0416S_1, DR0416S_2, DR0416S_3, DR02_1, DR02_2, DR02_3, DR12_1, DR12_2, DR12_3, DR33_1, DR33_2, DR33_3, DR21_1, DR21_2, DR21_3  # reconstruct test design from exemplary data, separately for each year and each domain data(trends) design<- by(data = trends, INDICES = trends[,c("year", "domain")], FUN = function (d) {          rownames(d) <- NULL          dw <- reshape2::dcast(unique(d[,c("booklet", "block", "pos")]), booklet~pos, value.var="block")          message("\nCondition: \n", eatTools::print_and_capture(d[1,c("year", "domain")]))          cl <- checkLinking(design=dw, bookletColumn ="booklet") } ) #>  #> Condition:  #>   year    domain #> 1 2010 listening #> Dataset is completely linked. #>  #> Condition:  #>   year    domain #> 1 2015 listening #> WARNING! Dataset is not completely linked. #> W A R N I N G !   Dataset is NOT completely linked (even if cases with missings on all items are removed). #>                   96 cases unconnected. Following items are unconnected:  #>                   Bl12_1, Bl12_2, Bl12_3, Bl09_1, Bl09_2, Bl09_3 #>  #> Condition:  #>   year    domain #> 1 2020 listening #> Dataset is completely linked. #>  #> Condition:  #>   year  domain #> 1 2010 reading #> Dataset is completely linked. #>  #> Condition:  #>   year  domain #> 1 2015 reading #> WARNING! Dataset is not completely linked. #> W A R N I N G !   Dataset is NOT completely linked (even if cases with missings on all items are removed). #>                   48 cases unconnected. Following items are unconnected:  #>                   Bl25_1, Bl25_2, Bl25_3 #>  #> Condition:  #>   year  domain #> 1 2020 reading #> Dataset is completely linked.

what: Prepares IRT analysis for Conquest, TAM, or mirt — defineModel
title: Prepares IRT analysis for Conquest, TAM, or mirt — defineModel
text: Facilitates data analysis using software Conquest, TAM, mirt. automatically checks data IRT consistency, generates Conquest syntax, label, anchor data files corresponding TAM/mirt call single model specified several arguments R. Finally, R object created contain required input Conquest TAM. start estimation, call runModel argument returned defineModel.

title: Prepares IRT analysis for Conquest, TAM, or mirt — defineModel
code: defineModel (dat, items, id, splittedModels = NULL,    irtmodel = c("1PL", "2PL", "PCM", "PCM2", "RSM", "GPCM", "GPCM.groups", "2PL.groups", "GPCM.design", "3PL"),    qMatrix=NULL, DIF.var=NULL, HG.var=NULL, group.var=NULL, weight.var=NULL, anchor = NULL,     domainCol=NULL, itemCol=NULL, valueCol=NULL, catCol = NULL, check.for.linking = TRUE, minNperItem = 50, removeMinNperItem = FALSE,    boundary = 6, remove.boundary = FALSE, remove.no.answers = TRUE, remove.no.answersHG = TRUE,     remove.missing.items = TRUE, remove.constant.items = TRUE, remove.failures = FALSE,     remove.vars.DIF.missing = TRUE, remove.vars.DIF.constant = TRUE,     verbose=TRUE, software = c("conquest","tam", "mirt"), dir = NULL, analysis.name,    schooltype.var = NULL, model.statement = "item",  compute.fit = TRUE,    pvMethod = c("regular", "bayesian"), fitTamMmlForBayesian = TRUE,    n.plausible=5, seed = NULL, conquest.folder= NULL,    constraints=c("cases","none","items"), std.err=c("quick","full","none"), distribution=c("normal","discrete"),    method=c("gauss", "quadrature", "montecarlo", "quasiMontecarlo"), n.iterations=2000,    nodes=NULL, p.nodes=2000, f.nodes=2000,converge=0.001,deviancechange=0.0001,    equivalence.table=c("wle","mle","NULL"), use.letters=FALSE,    allowAllScoresEverywhere = TRUE, guessMat = NULL, est.slopegroups = NULL,    fixSlopeMat = NULL, slopeMatDomainCol=NULL, slopeMatItemCol=NULL, slopeMatValueCol=NULL,     progress = NULL, Msteps = NULL, increment.factor=1 , fac.oldxsi=0,    export = list(logfile = TRUE, systemfile = FALSE, history = TRUE,    covariance = TRUE, reg_coefficients = TRUE, designmatrix = FALSE))

title: Prepares IRT analysis for Conquest, TAM, or mirt — defineModel
text: dat data frame containing variables necessary analysis. items Names column numbers variables item responses. Item response values must numeric (.e. 0, 1, 2, 3 ... ). Character values (.e. , B, C ... , b, c, ...) allowed. Class item columns expected numeric integer. Columns class character transformed. id Name column number identifier (ID) variable. splittedModels Optional: Object returned splitModels. Definition multiple model handling. irtmodel software = "conquest", argument ignored IRT model specified model.statement argument. software = "tam", argument irtmodel corresponds irtmodel argument tam.mml. See help page tam.mml details. software = "mirt", irtmodel data.frame two columns. First column: item identifier. Second column: Type response model item. See itemtype argument help file mirt details. Additionally, see example 10 specifying mixed model Rasch-, 2pl, partial credit, generalized partial credit items. qMatrix Optional: named data frame indicating items grouped dimensions. first column contains unique names items named “item”. columns contain dimension definitions named respective dimension names. positive value (e.g., 1 2 1.4) indicates loading weight item loads dimension, value 0 indicates respective item load dimension. q matrix specified user, unidimensional structure assumed. DIF.var Name column number one grouping variable differential item functioning analysis done. HG.var Optional: Names column numbers one context variables (e.g., sex, school). variables used latent regression model Conquest TAM. group.var Applies software = "tam". Optional: Optional: Names column numbers one grouping variables. Descriptive statistics WLEs Plausible Values computed separately group Conquest. weight.var Optional: Name column number one weighting variable. anchor Optional: named data frame anchor parameters. data.frame two columns, columns need named. case, item name first column. second column contains anchor parameters. Anchor items can subset items dataset vice versa. data frame contains two columns, columns must named explicitly using following arguments domainCol, itemCol, valueCol, catCol. domainCol Optional: necessary anchor argument used define anchor parameters. Moreover, specifying domainCol necessary, item identifiers anchor unique—example, specific item occurs two parameters, one domain-specific item parameter one additional “global” item parameter. domain column must specify parameter belongs domain. itemCol Optional: necessary anchor argument used define anchor parameters. Moreover, specifying itemCol necessary, anchor data frame two columns. itemCol column must specify column contains item identifier. valueCol Optional: necessary anchor argument used define anchor parameters. Moreover, specifying valueCol necessary, anchor data frame two columns. valueCol column must specify column contains item parameter values. catCol Optional: necessary anchor argument used define anchor parameters partial credit model. catCol column must specify column contains item parameter category indices. values column must named according convention "Cat1", "Cat2", "Cat3", . check..linking logical value indicating whether items dataset checked connected via design. minNperItem Numerical: message printed console item less valid values number defined minNperItem. removeMinNperItem Logical: Remove items less valid responses defined minNperItem? boundary Numerical: message printed console subject answered less number items defined boundary. remove.boundary Logical: Remove subjects answered less items defined boundary argument? remove..answers Logical: persons without item responses removed prior analysis? remove..answersHG Logical: persons without responses background variable removed prior analysis? remove.missing.items Logical: items without item responses removed prior analysis? remove.constant.items Logical: items without variance removed prior analysis? remove.failures Logical: persons without correct item response (.e., “0” responses) removed prior analysis? remove.vars.DIF.missing Logical: Applies DIF analyses. items without responses least one DIF group removed prior analyses? Note: Conquest may crash items remain data. remove.vars.DIF.constant Logical: Applies DIF analyses. items without variance least one DIF group removed prior analyses? Note: Conquest may crash items remain data. verbose logical value indicating whether messages printed R console. software desired estimation software analysis. dir directory output written . software = "conquest", dir must specified. software = "tam", dir mandatory. analysis.name character string specifying analysis name. software = "conquest", analysis.name must specified. Conquest input output files named analysis.name corresponding extensions. software = "tam", analysis.name mandatory. case multiple models estimation, split.models automatically defines analysis.name model. schooltype.var Optional: Name column number variable indicating school type (e.g. academic track, non-academic track). necessary p values computed school type separately. model.statement Optional: Applies software = "conquest". character string given model statement Conquest syntax. omitted, statement generated automatically respect defined model. compute.fit Applies software = "conquest". Compute item fit statistics? pvMethod Applies software = "tam": Specifies whether PVs drawn regularly using Bayesian algorithm. fitTamMmlForBayesian Logical, applies software = "tam": PVs drawn using Bayesian algorithm, necessary fit model via tam.mml . fitTamMmlForBayesian specifies whether model fitted though. See help page tam.pv.mcmc details. n.plausible number plausible values drawn conditioning model. seed Optional: Set seed value analysis. value used Conquest syntax file ('set seed'-statement, see conquest manual, p. 225) TAM (control$seed). Note seed occurs stochastic integration. koennen conquest.folder Applies software = "conquest". character string path name Conquest console, example "c:/programme/conquest/console_Feb2007.exe". package version 0.7.24 later, conquest executable file included package, user needn't specify argument. constraints character string specifying scale constrained. Possible options "cases" (default), "items" "none". anchor parameter specified anchor, constraints set "none" automatically. TAM option "none" allowed. (See help file tam.mml details.) std.err Applies software = "conquest". character string specifying type standard error estimated. Possible options "full", "quick" (default) "none". See Conquest manual pp.167 details standard error estimation. distribution Applies software = "conquest". character string indicating priori trait distribution. Possible options "normal" (default) "discrete". See Conquest manual pp.167 details population distributions. method character string indicating method used numerical stochastic integration. Possible options "gauss" (Gauss-Hermite quadrature: default), "quadrature" (Bock/Aitken quadrature) "montecarlo". See Conquest manual pp.167 details methods. using software = "tam", "gauss" "quadrature" essentially leads numerical integration, .e TAM called control$snodes = 0 control$nodes = seq(-6,6,len=nn), nn equals number nodes specified nodes argument defineModel (see ). Whether control$QMC set TRUE FALSE, depends whether "montecarlo" "quasiMontecarlo" chosen. "montecarlo" leads calling TAM control$QMC = FALSE snodes = nn, nn equals number nodes specified nodes argument defineModel. "quasiMontecarlo" leads calling TAM control$QMC = TRUE snodes = nn, nn equals number nodes specified nodes argument defineModel. met software = "tam" default (Quasi Monte Carlo integration control$QMC = TRUE), use software="tam", nodes = 21, method = "quasiMontecarlo". n.iterations integer value specifying maximum number iterations estimation proceed without improvement deviance. nodes integer value specifying number nodes used analysis. default value 20. using software = "tam", value specified leads calling TAM nodes = 20 snodes = 0 "gauss" "quadrature" "quasiMontecarlo" used method argument. "montecarlo" used method argument, value specified leads calling TAM control$snodes = 20. numerical integration, example, method = "gauss" nodes = 21 (TAM default) may appropriate. quasi monte carlo integration, method = "quasiMontecarlo" nodes = 1000 may appropriate (TAM authors recommend use least 1000 nodes). p.nodes Applies software = "conquest". integer value specifying number nodes used approximation posterior distributions, used drawing plausible values calculation EAP estimates. default value 2000. f.nodes Applies software = "conquest". integer value specifying number nodes used approximation posterior distributions calculation fit statistics. default value 2000. converge integer value specifying convergence criterion parameter estimates. estimation terminate largest change parameter estimate successive iterations EM algorithm less converge. default value 0.001. deviancechange integer value specifiying convergence criterion deviance. estimation terminate change deviance successive iterations EM algorithm less deviancechange. default value 0.0001. equivalence.table Applies software = "conquest". character string specifying type equivalence table print. precise, person estimator used create table must specified . Possible options "wle" (default), "mle" NULL. NULL, equivalence table computed . use.letters Applies software = "conquest". logical value indicating whether item response values coded letters. option can used partial credit models comprising items 10 categories avoid response columns width 2 Conquest. allowAllScoresEverywhere Applies software = "Conquest". Defines score statement generation multidimensional polytomous models. Consider two dimensions, `reading' `listening'. `reading', values 0, 1, 2, 3 occur. `listening', values 1, 2, 3, 4 occur. TRUE, values 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 defined dimensions. Otherwise, values 0, 1, 2, 3 defined `reading', values 1, 2, 3, 4 defined `listening'. guessMat Applies software = "tam" 3PL models. named data frame two columns indicating items common guessing parameter estimated. first column contains names items analysis named "item". second column numerical (integer values recommended) allocates items groups. group items, separate guessing parameter estimated. value second columns equals zero, guessing parameter fixed zero. est.slopegroups Applies software = "tam" 2PL models. Optionally, named data frame two columns indicating items common discrimination parameter estimated. first column contains names items analysis named "item". second column numerical (integer values recommended) allocates items groups. group items, separate discrimination parameter estimated. Without specifying est.slopegroups, discrimination parameter item estimated. fixSlopeMat Applies software = "tam" 2PL models. Optionally, named data frame two columns indicating items fixed discrimination assumed. first column contains names items discrimination fixed. Note item indicators unique—, use arguments slopeMatDomainCol, slopeMatItemCol slopeMatValueCol. second column numerical contains discrimination value. Note: date, works item dimensionality models. Within item dimensionality models must specified directly TAM, using B.fixed argument tam.mml. Items discrimation estimated occur data frame. slopeMatDomainCol Optional: necessary fixSlopeMat argument used define fixed slope parameters. Moreover, specifying slopeMatDomainCol necessary, item identifiers fixSlopeMat unique—example, specific item occurs two slope parameters, one domain-specific item slope parameter one additional “global” item parameter. domain column must specify parameter belongs domain. slopeMatItemCol Optional: necessary fixSlopeMat argument used define fixed slope parameters. Moreover, specifying itemCol necessary, fixSlopeMat data frame two columns. itemCol column must specify column contains item identifier. slopeMatValueCol Optional: necessary fixSlopeMat argument used define slope parameters. Moreover, specifying valueCol necessary, fixSlopeMat data frame two columns. valueCol column must specify column contains item parameter values. progress Logical: Applies software = "tam" software = "mirt". Print estimation progress messages console? NULL (default), progress printed software = "mirt", software = "tam". Msteps Number M steps item parameter estimation. high value M steps helpful cases non-convergence. default value 4; default 3pl models set 10. increment.factor Applies software = "tam". varied model converge. See help page tam.mml details. fac.oldxsi Applies software = "tam". varied model converge. See help page tam.mml details. export Applies software = "conquest". Specifies additional files written hard disk.

title: Prepares IRT analysis for Conquest, TAM, or mirt — defineModel
text: list contains information desired estimation. list intended processing via runModel. Structure list varies depending whether multiple models called using splitModels . splitModels called, number elements list equals number models defined via splitModels. element list list various elements: software Character string software intended use estimation, .e. "conquest" "tam" qMatrix Q matrix allocating items dimensions. .Names Named list relevant variables data set. dir Character string directory results saved. analysis.name Character string analysis' name. deskRes Data frame descriptives (e.g., p values) test items. discrim Data frame item discrimination values. perNA person identifiers examinees excluded analysis due solely missing values. per0 person identifiers examinees solely false responses. remove.failues set TRUE, persons excluded data set. perA person identifiers examinees solely correct responses. perExHG person identifiers examinees excluded analysis due missing values explicit variables. itemsExcluded Character string items excluded, example due zero variance solely missing values. software == "conquest", output additionally includes following elements: input Character string path Conquest input (cqc) file. conquest.folder Character string path conquest executable file. model.name Character string model name. software == "tam", output additionally includes following elements: anchor Optional: data frame anchor parameters (anchor parameters defined). daten prepared data TAM analysis. irtmodel Character string used IRT model. est.slopegroups Applies 2pl modeling. Information items share common slope parameter. guessMat Applies 3pl modeling. Information items share common guessing parameter. control List control parameters TAM estimation. n.plausible Desired number plausible values.

title: Prepares IRT analysis for Conquest, TAM, or mirt — defineModel
text: Sebastian Weirich

title: Prepares IRT analysis for Conquest, TAM, or mirt — defineModel
code: ################################################################################ ###               Preparation: necessary for all examples                    ### ################################################################################  # load example data # data set 'trends' contains item response data for three measurement occasions # in a single data.frame data(trends)  # second, create the q matrix from long format data qMat <- unique(trends[ ,c("item","domain")]) qMat <- data.frame ( qMat[,"item", drop=FALSE], model.matrix(~domain-1, data = qMat))   ################################################################################ ###                Example 1: Unidimensional Rasch Model                     ### ################################################################################  # Example 1: define and run a unidimensional Rasch model for the 2010 cohort # with all variables from booklet 02 (this booklet contains  reading and listening # items together). For the estimation, Conquest is used. # Since the wide-format dataset generated here will be used in later analyses, # several covariates - such as gender and SES - are generated at the same time. # These variables are not yet used in this example. datW <- subset(trends, booklet == "Bo02" & year == 2010) |>         reshape2::dcast(idstud+language+sex+ses+country~item, value.var = "value")  # defining the unidimensional model: specifying q matrix is not necessary mod1 <- defineModel(dat=datW, items= -c(1:5), id="idstud", analysis.name = "unidim", dir = tempdir()) #> Dataset is completely linked. #> 'gauss' has been chosen for estimation method. Number of nodes was not explicitly specified. Set nodes to 20. #> Q matrix specifies 1 dimension(s).  # run the model run1 <- runModel(mod1)  # get the results res1 <- getResults(run1) #> Cannot identify 'seed' from cqc file. #> Warning: running command '"quarto" TMPDIR=C:/Users/grewered/AppData/Local/Temp/Rtmp0GyuPo/file5a44534d2148 -V' had status 1 #> Found TERM 1: 'item'  #> Found 1 dimension(s):  #> Found 0 regressor(s). #> Warning: NAs introduced by coercion #> Pattern 'item' found. Assume no partial credit model. #> Cannot identify variable identifier for additional term 'regression' in file 'unidim.shw'. Skip procedure. #> Found valid WLEs of 172 person(s) for 1 dimension(s). #> 172 persons and 1 dimensions(s) found. #> 5 plausible values were drawn for each person on each dimension.  # extract the item parameters from the results object item1<- itemFromRes(res1)   ################################################################################ ###       Example 1a: Unidimensional Rasch Model with DIF estimation         ### ################################################################################  # nearly the same procedure as in example 1. Using 'sex' as DIF variable # note that 'sex' is a factor variable here. Conquest needs all explicit variables # to be numeric. Variables will be automatically transformed to numeric by # 'defineModels'. However, it might be the better idea to transform the variable # manually. datW[,"sexNum"] <- car::recode ( datW[,"sex"] , "'male'=0; 'female'=1", as.factor = FALSE)  # as we have defined a new variable ('sexNum') in the data, it is a good idea # to explicitly specify item columns ... instead of saying 'items= -c(1:5)' which # means: Everything except column 1 to 5 are item columns items<- grep("^T[[:digit:]]{2}", colnames(datW)) mod1a<- defineModel(dat=datW, items= items, id="idstud", DIF.var = "sexNum",         analysis.name = "unidimDIF", dir = tempdir()) #> '.', '-', and '_' nor upper case letters are allowed in explicit variable names and numbers in DIF variable name. Delete signs from variables names for explicit and DIF variables:  #>  #>               cols    old    new #>       DIF.var   39 sexNum sexnum #> Dataset is completely linked. #> 'gauss' has been chosen for estimation method. Number of nodes was not explicitly specified. Set nodes to 20. #> Q matrix specifies 1 dimension(s).  # run the model run1a<- runModel(mod1a)  # get the results res1a<- getResults(run1a) #> Cannot identify 'seed' from cqc file. #> Warning: running command '"quarto" TMPDIR=C:/Users/grewered/AppData/Local/Temp/Rtmp0GyuPo/file5a444ae2fe6 -V' had status 1 #> Found TERM 1: 'item'  #> Found TERM 2: '(-)sexnum'  #> Found TERM 3: 'item*sexnum'  #> There seem to be one more column than columns names. Expect missing column name before 'ESTIMATE'.  #> Check outputfile for term 'item*sexnum' in file: 'C:\Users\grewered\AppData\Local\Temp\Rtmp0GyuPo/unidimDIF.shw'.  #> Found 1 dimension(s):  #> Found 0 regressor(s). #> Warning: NAs introduced by coercion #> Pattern 'item-sexnum+item*sexnum' found. Assume no partial credit model. #> Cannot identify variable identifier for additional term 'regression' in file 'unidimDIF.shw'. Skip procedure. #> Found valid WLEs of 172 person(s) for 1 dimension(s). #> 172 persons and 1 dimensions(s) found. #> 5 plausible values were drawn for each person on each dimension.   ################################################################################ ### Example 1b: Rasch Model with DIF variable in customized model statement  ### ################################################################################  # builing on example 1a, the DIF variable should be used without interaction term # and without eliminating items which do not vary within DIF groups. We need the # 'sexNum' variable which was defined previously # Example based on 'model 6' in 'r:\Nawi\main\10_Studien\Auswertung\Alex Robitzsch R-Skripte\skalierung_conquest_mit_R_04.R' items<- grep("^T[[:digit:]]{2}", colnames(datW)) mod1b<- defineModel(dat=datW, items= items, id="idstud", model.statement = "item + sexNum",         analysis.name = "unidimCustom", dir = tempdir()) #> '.', '-', and '_' nor upper case letters are allowed in explicit variable names and numbers in DIF variable name. Delete signs from variables names for explicit and DIF variables:  #>  #>                cols    old    new #>       add.vars   39 sexNum sexnum #>     Remove deleted signs from variables names for explicit variables also in the model statement. Please check afterwards for consistency! #> Dataset is completely linked. #> 'gauss' has been chosen for estimation method. Number of nodes was not explicitly specified. Set nodes to 20. #> Q matrix specifies 1 dimension(s).  # run the model run1b<- runModel(mod1b)  # get the results res1b<- getResults(run1b) #> Cannot identify 'seed' from cqc file. #> Warning: running command '"quarto" TMPDIR=C:/Users/grewered/AppData/Local/Temp/Rtmp0GyuPo/file5a44587578a5 -V' had status 1 #> Found TERM 1: 'item'  #> Found TERM 2: 'sexnum'  #> Found 1 dimension(s):  #> Found 0 regressor(s). #> Warning: NAs introduced by coercion #> Pattern 'item+sexnum' found. Assume no partial credit model. #> Cannot identify variable identifier for additional term 'regression' in file 'unidimCustom.shw'. Skip procedure. #> Found valid WLEs of 172 person(s) for 1 dimension(s). #> 172 persons and 1 dimensions(s) found. #> 5 plausible values were drawn for each person on each dimension.  # item parameter it1b <- itemFromRes(res1b)   ################################################################################ ###        Example 2a: Multidimensional Rasch Model with anchoring           ### ################################################################################  # Example 2a: running a multidimensional Rasch model on a subset of items with latent # regression. Use item parameter from the first model as anchor parameters  # read in anchor parameters from the results object of the first example aPar <- itemFromRes(res1)[,c("item", "est")]  # defining the model: specifying q matrix now is necessary. # Please note that all latent regression variables have to be of class numeric. # If regression variables are factors, dummy variables automatically will be used. # (This behavior is equivalent as in lm() for example.) mod2a<- defineModel(dat=datW, items= grep("^T[[:digit:]]{2}", colnames(datW)),         id="idstud", analysis.name = "twodim", HG.var = c("language","sex", "ses"),         qMatrix = qMat, anchor = aPar, n.plausible = 20,dir = tempdir()) #> Following 250 item(s) missed in data frame will be removed from Q matrix:  #>     T07_08, T02_02, T07_01, T02_06, T07_05, T07_06, T02_07, T07_02, T07_04, T02_03, T07_09, T02_01, T02_04, T07_03, T02_05, T07_07, T07_10, T12_11, T09_12, T01_08, T13_15, T15_03, T13_04, T13_07, T13_16, T13_01,           0.08627933   ################################################################################ ###    Example 6: Scaling, linking and equating for multiple models (II)     ### ################################################################################  # Example 6 and 6a: define und run multiple models according to different domains  # (item groups) and different person groups. This example mimics the routines  # necessary for the 'Laendervergleich/Bildungstrend' at the Institute for  # Educational Progress (IQB). Example 6 demonstrates routines without trend  # estimation, i.e. for the first (or solely) measurement occasion. Example 6a # demonstrates routines for the second measurement occasion (t2) which is linked # to t1. Example 6b demonstrates routines for the third measurement occasion (t3) # which is linked to the common scale of t1 and t2.  # Preparation: assume time of measurement 't1' corresponds to the year 2010. # This is the year of the reference population. We consider both domains, # reading and listening in a single call. Hence, the data.frame 'datT1' contains # items of both domains. We add weights to the data as the definition of mean # and sd of the reference population (population of 't1') necessitates weights. datT1<- reshape2::dcast(subset ( trends, year == 2010),         idstud+country+sex+ses+language+wgt~item, value.var="value")  # generate Q matrix qMat <- unique(trends[ ,c("item","domain")]) qMat <- data.frame ( qMat[,"item", drop=FALSE], model.matrix(~domain-1, data = qMat))  # First step: item calibration in separate unidimensional models for each domain # (the models are short and simple, so we don't need multicore) modsT1<- splitModels ( qMatrix = qMat, nCores = 1) #> -------------------------------- #> splitModels: generating 2 models #> .. #> see <returned>$models #> number of cores: 1 #> --------------------------------  # define 2 models. Note: not all items of the Q matrix are present in the data. # Items which occur only in the Q matrix will be ignored.  defT1 <- defineModel(dat = datT1, id = "idstud", check.for.linking = TRUE,          splittedModels = modsT1, software = "tam") #>  #> Specification of 'qMatrix' and 'person.groups' results in 2 model(s). #> Warning: Model split preparation for model No. 1, model name Number of nodes was not explicitly specified. Set nodes to 20. #> Q matrix specifies 1 dimension(s).  # run (calibrate) the 2 models subsequently runT1 <- runModel(defT1)  # get the results of the two unidimensional models resT1 <- getResults(runT1, omitWle = TRUE, Q3 = FALSE) #> Getting standard errors with the tam.se function: 0.2 secs #> |*****| #> |-----| #> Getting PVs calling tam.pv from getTamPVs: 2 secs #> Getting standard errors with the tam.se function: 0.6 secs #> |*****| #> |-----| #> Getting PVs calling tam.pv from getTamPVs: 1.7 secs  # extract item parameters from the 'results' object # t1 is the reference measurement occasion, i.e. no linking/equating is necessary itemT1<- itemFromRes(resT1)  # The measurement point 't1' defines the reference population. We need to specify # mean and SD, using weights. eqRef <- equat1pl(resT1) #> Found 2 model(s). #>    Equating is executed for each dimension in each model separately. #> No norm parameter defined ('prmNorm' is missing). Treat current sample as drawn from the reference population.  # transformation to the 'bista' metric # Note: if the sample was drawn from the reference population in a weighted sample, # mean and SD are not yet known. So we ignore the 'refPop' argument in 'transformToBista' # and simply define the cut scores. The function then assumes that the parameter # stem from the reference population and estimates its mean and sd. cuts  <- list ( domainreading = list ( values = 390+0:3*75),          domainlistening = list ( values = 360+0:3*85)) tfRef <- transformToBista ( equatingList = eqRef, cuts=cuts, vera=FALSE,          weights = datT1[,c("idstud", "wgt")] ) #> 'refPop' was not defined. Treat current sample as drawn from the reference population. #> The 'refPop' data.frame does not include information about reference population mean/SD on Bista metric. Values will be defaulted to mean = 500 and SD = 100.  # Second step: drawing plausible values separately for each country. # A two-dimensional (reading/listening) model is specified separately for each # person group (= each country) with item parameters fixed at their calibration # values. Moreover, a latent regression model is used (in the actual 'Laendervergleich', # regressors are principal components of previously imputed background variables). We use 'sex', # 'ses' and 'language' as regressors. For convenience, 'ses' is scaled (mean = 0, sd = 1) datT1[,"ses_scaled"] <- scale(datT1[,"ses"])[,1]  # have a look at 'sex' and 'language at home' in each country: table(datT1[,c("country", "sex")]) #>           sex #> country    female male #>   countryA    787  811 #>   countryB    663  646 #>   countryC    795  774 table(datT1[,c("country", "language")]) #>           language #> country    native nativeAndOther other #>   countryA   1143            437    18 #>   countryB   1218             81    10 #>   countryC   1248            308    13  # Running second step: split models according to person groups # ('all.persons' must be FALSE, otherwise the whole group would be treated as # a separate distinct group.) modT1P<- splitModels ( person.groups = datT1[,c("idstud", "country")],          all.persons = FALSE, nCores = 1) #> -------------------------------- #> splitModels: generating 3 models #> ... #> see <returned>$models #> number of cores: 1 #> --------------------------------  # define the 3 country-specific 2-dimensional models, specifying latent regression # model and fixed item parameters. defT1P<- defineModel(dat = datT1, items = itemT1[,"item"], id = "idstud",          check.for.linking = TRUE, splittedModels = modT1P, qMatrix = qMat,          anchor = itemT1[,c("item", "est")],          HG.var = c("sex", "ses_scaled", "language"),  software = "tam") #>  #> Cannot find log file 'C:\Users\grewered\AppData\Local\Temp\Rtmp0GyuPo/dif_pcm.log'. #> Cannot identify 'seed' from cqc file. #> Error in eatTools::halveString(dev, "\\."): Assertion on 'string' failed: Must be of type 'character', not 'NULL'. it  <- itemFromRes(resC) #> Error: object 'resC' not found shw <- get.shw(file.path(tempdir(), "dif_pcm.shw"), dif.term = "item*sex") #> Warning: cannot open file 'C:\Users\grewered\AppData\Local\Temp\Rtmp0GyuPo/dif_pcm.shw': No such file or directory #> Error in file(file, "r"): cannot open the connection   ################################################################################ ###    Example 12: Norm study within the realm of partial credit models      ### ################################################################################  # The following example mimics the steps the required to norm the educational standards # when the underlying model is a partial credit model.  # load partial credit long format data. This large scale assessment data surveyed in # an incomplete block design is representative of a domain covered by the new standards. # We assume thta the sample was drawn from the norm population (reference population) data(reading)  # transform the data to the wide format datW <- reshape2::dcast(reading[which(reading[,"type"] != "iglu"),],idstud+sex+language+country~item, value.var = "valueSum")  # combine the two lowest categories to avoid categories with too few observations datW[,"D205143"] <- car::recode(datW[,"D205143"], "1=0; 2=1; 3=2; 4=3; 5=4")  # partial credit model. We draw 15 plausible values to achieve greater precision # in estimating the distribution of individuals. def1 <- defineModel(dat=datW, items = -c(1:4), id=1, irtmodel = "PCM", software="tam", nodes = 21, n.plausible=15) #> 7 variable(s) are not strictly dichotomous with 0/1 ... Expect a rating scale model or partial credit model. #>    Items(s) 'D025033', 'D201113', 'D204013', 'D205143', 'D224053', 'D225113': 0, 1, 2, 3, 4      #>    Items(s) 'D223143':                                                        0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5   #> 13 subject(s) do not solve any item: #>    P0007 (12 false), P0837 (13 false), P1569 (49 false) ...  #> 20 subject(s) solved each item: P0968 (11 correct), P0843 (13 correct), P1047 (29 correct) ...  #> Dataset is completely linked. #> Q matrix specifies 1 dimension(s). run1 <- runModel(def1)  # use ntheta = 40000 for higher precision res1 <- getResults(run1, ntheta = 40000, theta.model = FALSE) #> Getting standard errors with the tam.se function: 0.6 secs #> Getting infit parameters calling tam.fit from getTamInfit: 0.2 secs #> Getting WLEs calling tam.wle from getTamWles: 0.6 secs #> |***************| #> |---------------| #> Getting PVs calling tam.pv from getTamPVs: 37 secs #> Getting Q3 statistic calling tam.modelfit from getTamQ3: 0.5 secs  # estimate mean and standard deviation of the reference population from the plausible values # use the long format by toWideFormat = FALSE. Load the eatRep package for mean/SD computation library(eatRep) pv1  <- pvFromRes(res1, toWideFormat = FALSE) mSD  <- repMean(datL = pv1, ID="idstud", imp="imp", dependent="value") #> 1 analyse(s) overall according to: 'group.splits = 0'. #> Assume unnested structure with 15 imputations. #>  mSDR <- report2(mSD, round = FALSE)[["plain"]]; dim(mSDR) #> [1]  3 10  # use mean/SD to transform the item and person parameters. Since the cur scores have not yet # been established - as they are still being defined in the standard-setting process - we are # assigning arbitrary values for now. Since the sample can be considered as being drawn from # the reference population, no equating procedure is necessary. The function is therefore # simply passed through. The mean and standard deviation of the educational standards metric # are set to 500 and 100, respectively. eq   <- equat1pl(res1) #> Found 1 model(s). #>    Equating is executed for each dimension in each model separately. #> No norm parameter defined ('prmNorm' is missing). Treat current sample as drawn from the reference population. trnsf<- transformToBista(equatingList=eq, refPop= data.frame(domain="Dim1",         m = mSDR[which(mSDR[,"parameter"] == "mean"),"est"],         sd = mSDR[which(mSDR[,"parameter"] == "sd"),"est"]),         cuts = list(Dim1 = list(values = c(400,600))), vera=FALSE) #> The 'refPop' data.frame does not include information about reference population mean/SD on Bista metric. Values will be defaulted to mean = 500 and SD = 100. #> Warning: Skip check whether all competence levels are occupied (due to bayesian plausible values imputation).

title: Read Conquest WLE or MLE Output Files — get.wle

what: Collect all results from Conquest, TAM, or mirt analyses into a common data frame — getResults
title: Collect all results from Conquest, TAM, or mirt analyses into a common data frame — getResults
text: First IRT model defined using defineModel. Afterwards, call runModel argument returned defineModel start estimation. last step create results frame using getResults.

title: Collect all results from Conquest, TAM, or mirt analyses into a common data frame — getResults
code: getResults( runModelObj, overwrite = FALSE, Q3 = TRUE, q3theta = c("pv", "wle", "eap"),              q3MinObs = 0, q3MinType = c("singleObs", "marginalSum"), omitFit = FALSE,              omitRegr = FALSE, omitWle = FALSE, omitPV = FALSE, abs.dif.bound = 0.6,              sig.dif.bound = 0.3, p.value = 0.9, nplausible = NULL, ntheta = 2000,             normal.approx = FALSE, samp.regr = FALSE, theta.model=FALSE, np.adj=8,             group = NULL, beta_groups = TRUE, level = .95, n.iter = 1000,             n.burnin = 500, adj_MH = .5, adj_change_MH = .05, refresh_MH = 50,              accrate_bound_MH = c(.45, .55),  sample_integers=FALSE, theta_init=NULL,             print_iter = 20, verbose = TRUE, calc_ic=TRUE, omitUntil = 1, seed=NA)

title: Collect all results from Conquest, TAM, or mirt analyses into a common data frame — getResults
text: runModelObj object returned runModel. overwrite Logical. result files overwritten exist? Q3 Logical. Estimate Q3 statistic according Yen (1984)? Note: possible uni-dimensional models. software == "tam", Q3 statistic estimated using tam.modelfit function. software == "Conquest", Q3 statistic estimated using Q3 function sirt package. Note Q3 estimation work yet software == "mirt". q3theta Specify whether Q3 statistic estimated using PVs, WLEs EAPs theta variable. q3MinObs Q3 statistic might untrustworthy item covariance estimation based observations. Define minimum number observation fulfilled Q3 estimation. q3MinType "singleObs", q3MinObs argument based least number observations \(2\times 2\) 0/1 frequency table item pairs. "marginalSum", q3MinObs argument based sum marginals \(2\times 2\) 0/1 frequency table item pairs. omitFit Logical. item fit values included results? omitRegr Logical. regression parameters standard errors included results? omitWle Logical. WLE estimates included results? omitPV Logical. plausible values included results? abs.dif.bound Applies DIF analyses performed . DIF-Parameter evaluated, specifies critical value absolute DIF. See details section information. sig.dif.bound Applies DIF analyses performed . DIF-Parameter evaluated, specifies critical value confidence interval DIF. See details section information. p.value Applies DIF analyses performed . DIF-Parameter evaluated, specifies critical p-value confidence interval DIF. See details section information. nplausible Applies software = "tam": Number plausible values drawn. Note: number plausible values already defined defineModel, Conquest needs know number PVs prior estimation. TAM, possible redefine number plausible values overwrite definition given defineModel. ntheta Applies software = "tam". Following description borrowed help file tam.pv TAM package: Number ability nodes plausible value imputation. Note function ability nodes simulated whole sample, every person (contrary software Conquest). normal.approx Applies software = "tam". Following description borrowed help file tam.pv TAM package: optional logical indicating whether individual posterior distributions approximated normal distribution? default FALSE. case normal.approx=TRUE (normal distribution approximation), number ability nodes ntheta can substantially smaller 2000, say 200 500. normal approximation implemented unidimensional multidimensional models. samp.regr Applies software = "tam". Following description borrowed help file tam.pv TAM package: optional logical indicating whether regression coefficients fixed plausible value imputation also sampled posterior distribution? default FALSE. Sampled regression coefficients obtained nonparametric bootstrap. theta.model Applies software = "tam". Following description borrowed help file tam.pv TAM package: Logical indicating whether theta grid tamobj object used plausible value imputation. case normal.approx=TRUE, sufficient many applications. np.adj Applies software = "tam". Following description borrowed help file tam.pv TAM package: parameter defines “spread” random theta values drawing plausible values normal.approx=FALSE. \(s_{EAP}\) denotes standard deviation posterior distribution theta (one-dimensional case), theta simulated normal distribution standard deviation np.adj times \(s_{EAP}\). group Applies software = "tam" pvMethod = "bayesian". Optional vector group identifiers. See help page tam.pv.mcmc details. beta_groups Applies software = "tam" pvMethod = "bayesian". See help page tam.pv.mcmc details. level Applies software = "tam" pvMethod = "bayesian". Confidence level bayesian approach. See help page oftam.pv.mcmc details. n.iter Applies software = "tam" pvMethod = "bayesian". Number iterations bayesian approach. See help page tam.pv.mcmc details. n.burnin Applies software = "tam" pvMethod = "bayesian". Number burn-iterations bayesian approach. See help page tam.pv.mcmc details. adj_MH Applies software = "tam" pvMethod = "bayesian". See help page tam.pv.mcmc details. adj_change_MH Applies software = "tam" pvMethod = "bayesian". See help page tam.pv.mcmc details. refresh_MH Applies software = "tam" pvMethod = "bayesian". See help page tam.pv.mcmc details. accrate_bound_MH Applies software = "tam" pvMethod = "bayesian". See help page tam.pv.mcmc details. sample_integers Applies software = "tam" pvMethod = "bayesian". Logical indicating whether weights complete cases sampled bootstrap. See help page tam.pv.mcmc details. theta_init Applies software = "tam" pvMethod = "bayesian". Optional matrix initial theta values. See help page tam.pv.mcmc details. print_iter Applies software = "tam" pvMethod = "bayesian". See help page tam.pv.mcmc details. verbose Applies software = "tam" pvMethod = "bayesian". See help page tam.pv.mcmc details. calc_ic Applies software = "tam" pvMethod = "bayesian". Logical indicating whether information criteria computed. See help page tam.pv.mcmc details. omitUntil Argument passed plotDevianceConquest: optional value indicating number iterations omitted plotting. seed Fixed simulation seed. value directly passed tam.fit function.

title: Collect all results from Conquest, TAM, or mirt analyses into a common data frame — getResults
text: defineModel run software Conquest, path argument ('dir') necessary. path argument optional software TAM. 'dir' specified, getResults additionally writes output specified folder, using analysis.name argument file naming. Otherwise, getResults returnes result data frame. DIF analyses performed , user can specify criteria according DIF interpreted evaluated. default, ETS criteria (Zieky, 1993) used classify DIF three distinct categories, "", "B", "C". Small DIF ("") corresponds absolute DIF values .43 (significance test performed ); medium DIF ("B") corresponds absolute DIF values .43 .64 significantly different zero. High DIF ("C") corresponds absolute DIF values .64 significantly different .43 (DeMars, 2011; Monahan et al. 2007). Alternatively, three arguments abs.dif.bound, sig.dif.bound, p.value allow specify user-defined dichotomous criteria. items flagged DIF, absolute value increases 0.5 significantly exceeds 0.1 alpha level 0.05, use abs.dif.bound = 0.5 sig.dif.bound = 0.1 p.value = 0.95.

title: Collect all results from Conquest, TAM, or mirt analyses into a common data frame — getResults
text: data frame long format ten columns. model name model (specified user analysis.name. source estimation software (.e, conquest TAM) var1 variable name corresponding value given, .e. indicator. var2 Additional variable information necessary. type Type coefficient (example, random fixed). indicator.group type group corresponding variable belongs . group group corresponding variable belongs . Note: group nested within indicator.group. par type parameter. derived.par Optionally: derived parameter. value value corresponding estimate.

title: Collect all results from Conquest, TAM, or mirt analyses into a common data frame — getResults
text: DeMars, C. E. (2011). analytic comparison effect sizes differential item functioning. Applied Measurement Education, 24 (3), 189-209. https://doi.org/10.1080/08957347.2011.580255 Monahan, P. O., McHorney, C. ., Stump, T. E. & Perkins, . J. (2007). Odds ratio, delta, ETS classification, standardization measures DIF magnitude binary logistic regression. Journal Educational Behavioral Statistics, 32 (1), 92-109. https://doi.org/10.3102/1076998606298035 Zieky, M. (1993). Practical questions use DIF statistics item development. P. W. Holland & H. Wainer (Eds.), Differential item functioning (pp. 337-347). Hillsdale, NJ: Lawrence Erlbaum.

what: 1pl linking errors for three measurement occasions — multiEquatError
title: 1pl linking errors for three measurement occasions — multiEquatError
text: Function combines linking errors based 1pl linking procedure according equat1pl three measurement occasions.

title: 1pl linking errors for three measurement occasions — multiEquatError
code: multiEquatError (eq.1_2, eq.2_3, eq.1_3, dependentDIF =FALSE, verbose=TRUE)

title: 1pl linking errors for three measurement occasions — multiEquatError
text: eq.1_2 object returned equat1pl linking first second measurement occasion. eq.2_3 object returned equat1pl linking second third measurement occasion. eq.1_3 object returned equat1pl linking first third measurement occasion. dependentDIF Logical. DIF assumed correlated measurement occasions one two versus two three, covariance taken account. assumption linear dependency seem reasonable, option possibly results overfitting underestimation true linking error. verbose Logical: print linking informations console?

title: 1pl linking errors for three measurement occasions — multiEquatError
text: list data.frames linking errors, according number domains. output intended used argument replaceLinkingError function.

title: 1pl linking errors for three measurement occasions — multiEquatError
text: Sebastian Weirich

title: 1pl linking errors for three measurement occasions — multiEquatError
text: Monseur, C., & Berezner, . (2007). Computation Equating Errors International Surveys Education. Journal Applied Measurement 8(3): 323-335.

title: 1pl linking errors for three measurement occasions — multiEquatError
code: data(trends)  ################################################################################ ###    Example 1: Linking for three measurement occasions and one domain     ### ################################################################################  # calibrate all three measurements, using a unidimensional model each # (only reading, no testlets, no jackknife) results <- by(data = trends, INDICES = trends[,"year"], FUN = function (y){            dat <- reshape2::dcast(subset ( y, domain == "reading"), idstud~item, value.var="value")            def <- defineModel(dat=dat, items= -1, id="idstud", software="tam")            run <- runModel(def)            res <- getResults(run)            return(res)}) #> 17 subject(s) do not solve any item: #>    P00173 (6 false), P01137 (7 false), P02863 (23 false) ...  #> 97 subject(s) solved each item: P00078 (6 correct), P00389 (10 correct), P03338 (27 correct) ...  #> Dataset is completely linked. #> 'gauss' has been chosen for estimation method. Number of nodes was not explicitly specified. Set nodes to 20. #> Q matrix specifies 1 dimension(s). #> Getting standard errors with the tam.se function: 0.4 secs #> Getting WLEs calling tam.wle from getTamWles: 0.7 secs #> |*****| #> |-----| #> Getting PVs calling tam.pv from getTamPVs: 1.7 secs #> Getting Q3 statistic calling tam.modelfit from getTamQ3: 0.5 secs #> 56 subject(s) do not solve any item: #>    P04511 (6 false), P05616 (10 false), P08143 (14 false) ...  #> 114 subject(s) solved each item: P04509 (6 correct), P08241 (8 correct), P05805 (20 correct) ...  #> W A R N I N G ! lErrors <- multiEquatError(eq.1_2, eq.2_3, eq.1_3) #>  #> Dimension 'Dim1': Direct linking error of the three combinations of measurements:  #>         mzp1 mzp2 N.Items    SD   Var linkerror chained mzp_1.vs.3 mzp_1.vs.2.vs.3 #>       1    1    2      64 0.234 0.055     0.029      NA         NA              NA #>       2    1    3      63 0.295 0.087     0.037   0.038      0.325           0.251 Following code demonstrates the replacement of old linking error in the 'equatingList' object # for further processing # direct linking 1 to 3 (1 is reference) eq1.vs.3<- equat1pl(results[[3]], prmNorm = items[[1]][,c("item", "est")], difBound = 0.64, iterativ = TRUE) #> Found 1 model(s). #>    Equating is executed for each dimension in each model separately. #>  #> ==================================================================================================== #>   #> Model No. 1 #>     Model name:                not_specified #>     Number of dimension(s):    1 #>     Name(s) of dimension(s):   Dim1 #>     Number of linking items:   68 #>     Linking method:            Mean.Mean #>  #> Dimension 'Dim1': 4 of 68 items with linking |DIF| > 0.64 identified. #>    Iteration 1: Exclude item 'T01_07'. #>    Iteration 2: Exclude item 'T02_07'. #>    Iteration 3: Exclude item 'T09_07'. #>    Iteration 4: Exclude item 'T04_04'. #>    Iteration 5: Exclude item 'T07_10'. #>  #>     method iter itemExcluded DIF.excluded linking.constant linkerror #> 1 iterativ    0                                     -0.305     0.048 #> 2 iterativ    1       T01_07        1.656           -0.280     0.041 #> 3 iterativ    2       T02_07       -0.849           -0.293     0.040 #> 4 iterativ    3       T09_07       -0.735           -0.304     0.039 #> 5 iterativ    4       T04_04        -0.67           -0.314     0.038 #> 6 iterativ    5       T07_10       -0.644           -0.325     0.037 #>   # replace 'direct' linking error with 'indirect' linking error from 'multiEquatError()' eq1.vs.3<- replaceLinkingError (equatingList=eq1.vs.3, multiEquatError_output=lErrors) #> Dimension 'Dim1': Replace old linking error 0.0372 with 0.0378   ################################################################################ ###    Example 2: Linking for three measurement occasions and two domains    ### ################################################################################  # calibrate all three measurements, using a unidimensional model each # reading and listening are handled subsequently, using the model split function results2<- by(data = trends, INDICES = trends[,"year"], FUN = function (y){            qmat<- unique(y[ ,c("item","domain")])            qmat<- data.frame ( qmat[,"item", drop=FALSE], model.matrix(~domain-1, data = qmat))            spl <- splitModels ( qMatrix = qmat,  nCores = 1)            dat <- reshape2::dcast(y, idstud~item, value.var="value")            def <- defineModel(dat=dat, splittedModels = spl, id="idstud", software="tam")            run <- runModel(def)            res <- getResults(run)            return(res)}) #> -------------------------------- #> splitModels: generating 2 models #> .. #> see <returned>$models #> number of cores: 1 #> -------------------------------- #>  #> Specification of 'qMatrix' and 'person.groups' results in 2 model(s). #>  #>  #> ========================================= #> Model No. 1 #>     Model name:           domainlistening #>     Number of items:      51 #>     Number of persons:    4476 #>     Number of dimensions: 1 #> ========================================= #>  #> 76 subject(s) do not solve any item: #>    P00009 (6 false), P00864 (6 false), P02759 (21 false) ...  #> 55 subject(s) solved each item: P00099 (10 correct), P00404 (10 correct), P02659 (11 correct) ...  #> Dataset is completely linked. #> 'gauss' has been chosen for estimation method. pairwise linking eq.1_2 <- equat1pl(e1, e2,difBound = 0.64, iterativ = TRUE) #>  #> ==================================================================================================== #>   #> Model No. NA #>     Model name:                Dim1 #>     Number of dimension(s):    1 #>     Name(s) of dimension(s):   Dim1 #>     Number of linking items:   3 #>     Linking method:            Mean.Mean #>  #>   linking.constant linkerror #> 1           -0.144     0.138 #>  eq.2_3 <- equat1pl(e2, e3,difBound = 0.64, iterativ = TRUE) #>  #> ==================================================================================================== #>   #> Model No. NA #>     Model name:                Dim1 #>     Number of dimension(s):    1 #>     Name(s) of dimension(s):   Dim1 #>     Number of linking items:   5 #>     Linking method:            Mean.Mean #>  #>   linking.constant linkerror #> 1           -0.072     0.117 #>  eq.1_3 <- equat1pl(e1, e3,difBound = 0.64, iterativ = TRUE) #>  #> ==================================================================================================== #>   #> Model No. NA #>     Model name:                Dim1 #>     Number of dimension(s):    1 #>     Name(s) of dimension(s):   Dim1 #>     Number of linking items:   4 #>     Linking method:            Mean.Mean #>  #>   linking.constant linkerror #> 1           -0.149     0.079 #>  multiEquatError(eq.1_2, eq.2_3, eq.1_3) #>  #> Dimension 'Dim1': Direct linking error of the three combinations of measurements:  #>         mzp1 mzp2 N.Items    SD   Var linkerror chained mzp_1.vs.3 mzp_1.vs.2.vs.3 #>       1    1    2       3 0.238 0.057     0.138      NA         NA              NA #>       2    1    3       4 0.158 0.025     0.079    0.18     -0.149          -0.216 #>       3    2    3       5 0.261 0.068     0.117      NA         NA              NA #> $Dim1 #>      trend31  trend3221       le31    le3221 #> 1 -0.1488454 -0.2160025 0.07918052 0.1804314 #>

code: data(trends) # choose only 2010 dat <- trends[which(trends[,"year"] == 2010),] # choose reading dat <- dat[which(dat[,"domain"] == "reading"),]  # first reshape the data set into wide format datW <- reshape2::dcast(dat, idstud~item, value.var="value")  # defining the model: specifying q matrix is not necessary mod1 <- defineModel(dat=datW, items= -1, id="idstud", software = "tam") #> 17 subject(s) do not solve any item: #>    P00173 (6 false), P01137 (7 false), P02863 (23 false) ...  #> 97 subject(s) solved each item: P00078 (6 correct), P00389 (10 correct), P03338 (27 correct) ...  #> Dataset is completely linked. #> 'gauss' has been chosen for estimation method. Number of nodes was not explicitly specified. Set nodes to 20. #> Q matrix specifies 1 dimension(s).  # run the model run1 <- runModel(mod1)  # get the results res1 <- getResults(run1) #> Getting standard errors with the tam.se function: 0.5 secs #> Getting WLEs calling tam.wle from getTamWles: 0.6 secs #> |*****| #> |-----| #> Getting PVs calling tam.pv from getTamPVs: 1.8 secs #> Getting Q3 statistic calling tam.modelfit from getTamQ3: 0.5 secs  # plot for one item  plotICC  ( resultsObj = res1, defineModelObj = mod1, items = "T04_04") #> Note: To date, only 1pl/2pl dichotomous models are supported.

title: Computes equivalence table for the Rasch and partial credit model — simEquiTable
text: anchor data frame anchor parameters logit scale, transformed   metric reference population. Data frame must least two   columns: first column contains names anchored items. second   column contains anchor parameters. equivalence table generated   partial credit model, additional column respective item category   must specified data.frame. data.frame three columns.   case, additional arguments item, cat, value   must specified. various item parameters partial credit item must   therefore listed one data.frame. See second example   details. item Optional: Give number name item identifier column anchor. necessary partial credit models. cat Optional: Give number name category column anchor. necessary partial credit models. value Optional: Give number name parameter column anchor. necessary partial credit models. mRef Scalar: mean reference population. sdRef Scalar: Standard deviation reference population. addConst Additive constant parameter transformation. multConst Multiplicative constant parameter transformation. cutScores Named list one two elements. "values" numeric vector cut scores (increasing), "labels" optional character vector cut score labels. Note "labels" (specified) one length "values".

title: Computes equivalence table for the Rasch and partial credit model — simEquiTable
text: list two data frames, including complete table reduced table following 5 columns. score Students raw score est Estimated individual WLE according raw score. bista Transformed WLE ks competence level

title: Computes equivalence table for the Rasch and partial credit model — simEquiTable
text: Johannes Schult, Sebastian Weirich

title: Computes equivalence table for the Rasch and partial credit model — simEquiTable
code: ### Example 1: equivalence table for Rasch models # read anchor parameter file <- system.file("extdata", "results.rda", package = "eatModel") load(file)  # use domain 'reading' prm  <- subset(itemFromRes(res), model == "komplesen")  # use bista cut scores cuts   <- list ( values = 390+0:3*75, labels = c("I", "II", "III", "IV", "V") )  # create the equivalence table ret <- simEquiTable( anchor = prm[,c("item", "est")], cutScores = cuts , mRef = 0.039, sdRef = 1.071)  ### Example 2: equivalence table for partial credit model # load partial credit data data(reading)  # To speed up the process, the tests will be administered only for Test # Booklet 8. This booklet contains questions with some partial credit items. d   <- subset(reading, bookletID == "TH08") dw  <- reshape2::dcast(d, idstud~item, value.var = "valueSum") defT<- defineModel(dat = dw, items = -1, id = "idstud",  irtmodel = "PCM",software="tam") #> 4 variable(s) are not strictly dichotomous with 0/1 ... Expect a rating scale model or partial credit model. #>    Items(s) 'D025033', 'D204013', 'D225113': 0, 1, 2, 3, 4      #>    Items(s) 'D205143':                       0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5   #> Dataset is completely linked. #> 'gauss' has been chosen for estimation method. Number of nodes was not explicitly specified. Set nodes to 20. #> Q matrix specifies 1 dimension(s). runT<- runModel(defT) resT<- getResults(runT, omitPV=TRUE, Q3 = FALSE) it  <- itemFromRes(resT)  # create equivalence table with arbitrary cuts and reference values ret2<- simEquiTable( anchor = it, item = "item", cat="category", value = "est",        cutScores = list ( values = c(400, 600)), mRef = 0.047, sdRef = 1.181) #> Error in simEquiTable(anchor = it, item = "item", cat = "category", value = "est",     cutScores = list(values = c(400, 600)), mRef = 0.047, sdRef = 1.181): object 'it' not found
